---
title: "wrongful-erasure"
format: html
---

---
title: "Wrongful Erasure and Rightful Recognition"
subtitle: "Native Peoples of Virginia and the Struggle for Federally Recognized Sovereignty in the Twenty-First Century"
author: "David T. Watkeys"
date: "2023"
---

---
title: "Papers"
---

This page collects my scholarly writing from undergraduate through my Masters 
program at Virginia Tech. My work spans Indigenous history, legal history, 
and digital history — with new work produced every year since 2023.

---

## The Long Peyote Road (2025-2026)

*Masters Journal Article — Virginia Tech Department of History*

My Masters journal article traces the six-thousand-year history of Peyote 
religions among Indigenous peoples of North America, examining the tension 
between colonial suppression and Indigenous resistance. The article develops 
the concept of a **"repertoire of resistance"** — a set of strategies and 
tools that Peyotist communities have used to survive and adapt across centuries 
of colonization.

[Read Paper](papers/journal_article_the_long_peyote_road_website.pdf){target="_blank"}

---

## Cold War Collateral Damage: A Navajo Chronicle (2024)

*Undergraduate Digital History Project — Virginia Tech Department of History*

A digital history project examining the impact of Cold War policy on Navajo 
communities, combining prose, photography, and primary source links. 
Full migration to this site in progress.

[View Original Site](https://watkeyswordpress.vt.domains/LandingZone/){target="_blank"}

---

## Wrongful Erasure and Rightful Recognition (2023)

*Undergraduate Research Paper — Virginia Tech Department of History*

**Winner of the 2023 Gallagher History Prize ($10,000)**

This paper examines the long struggle of Virginia's Native peoples for federal 
recognition — systematically denied through legal mechanisms designed to erase 
Indigenous identity. Tracing the political and legal battles of the 
twenty-first century, the paper argues that federal recognition represents 
not just a bureaucratic designation but an act of rightful historical correction.

[Read Paper](papers/wrongful_erasure_and_rightful_recognition_website.pdf){target="_blank"}
